name: Mrs. f Marbles
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300°F.
2. In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, and salt until well combined.
3. Set aside.
4. In a large bowl using an electric mixer on medium speed, combine the sugars and butter. Beat until the mixture is creamy and light in color.
5. Add egg, sour cream, and vanilla extract to the sugar-butter mixture. Continue beating on medium speed until the batter is smooth and well combined.
6. Scrape down the sides of the bowl.
7. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, mixing at a low speed until just combined. Do not overmix.
8. Line ungreased cookie sheets with parchment paper or silicone baking mats.
9. Melt chocolate chips: Place them in a double boiler set over simmering (not boiling) water, stirring constantly. Alternatively, microwave on 50% power, stirring every 20 seconds until melted.
10. Allow the melted chocolate to cool slightly and pour it evenly over the cookie batter, creating small puddles or drops about 1-inch apart.
11. Using a wooden spoon or rubber spatula, gently fold the melted chocolate into the dough, incorporating only a few pieces of chocolate at a time.
12. Drop rounded tablespoons of the mixture onto the prepared cookie sheets.
13. Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until lightly golden and set around the edges but still soft in the center. Do not allow to brown.
14. Remove from oven and let cool on the baking sheets for a few minutes before transferring the cookies to a wire rack to cool completely.
